prompt:
Create a vertical 2:3 cinematic fantasy character-design poster for a guardian spirit called a Stand, blending ethereal Chinese typography with soft fashion photography and mythic creature design. The main subject is Higanbana · Wu, a young woman seated on a rough stone ledge beside a misty pond or stream, wearing a white ruched floral mini dress with thin straps and an oversized off-white knit cardigan slipping from one shoulder. Her hair is medium brown, loosely tied in a low messy bun with soft strands around the face; pose is elegant, quiet, slightly melancholic, hands resting on the ledge, legs angled forward. Hide or softly blur the face if needed, keeping the mood anonymous and poetic.

Behind and above her right shoulder, create the Stand: a large translucent white dragon-deer / fox-dragon guardian spirit with an elongated delicate snout, flowing feather-like mane, branching antler or horn shapes, veil-like fins, silk ribbons, dangling gold chains and tassels, and many small white flowers blooming along its head and body. It should feel like fog, petals, and moonlight given animal form: beautiful, fragile, protective, slightly uncanny, not cartoonish. The spirit curves around the woman as if guarding her, occupying the upper-right half of the poster.

Environment and lighting: a shaded waterside garden with drooping willow branches, dark green foliage, reflective water, floating mist, translucent gauze ribbons, tiny glowing petals or fireflies, and warm morning backlight filtering through haze. Use a palette of ivory, pearl white, champagne beige, pale blush, muted moss green, smoky gray, and soft gold. Overall atmosphere: serene, romantic, spectral, elegant, dreamlike, with a faint sense of grief and distance.

Design the poster as an information-rich Stand profile sheet with refined white serif typography and thin decorative rules. Include exactly 6 text blocks: 1) upper-left small label in Chinese and English, “替身設定 / STAND NAME”; 2) large title “彼岸花・霧” with smaller romanization “Higanbana · Wu”; 3) a short poetic Chinese paragraph beneath the title; 4) mid-left section titled “特殊能力” with ability name “花影迷離” and romanization “Hua Ying Mi Li,” followed by a concise ability description; 5) bottom-left section titled “設計關聯” with several short bullet-like Chinese lines and exactly 7 circular color swatches in ivory, cream, warm beige, dusty pink, gray-green, deep moss, and taupe; 6) bottom-right quote box containing “「你看到的美好，也許只是我編織的夢。」” plus a smaller boxed profile headed “少女檔案” with brief attributes such as personality, image, aura, and preferences. Add one narrow vertical Chinese text panel on the right side over the spirit, framed by thin lines. Text should be decorative and readable enough to evoke a luxury editorial character sheet, without overwhelming the image.

Visual style: high-end fantasy editorial poster, ultra-detailed, soft-focus photorealism mixed with delicate illustration, translucent layers, bokeh, shallow depth of field, fine ornamental linework, elegant Chinese book-design aesthetics, no harsh neon colors, no modern UI, no watermark. Use 彼岸花・霧 as the largest title, 花影迷離 as the ability heading, and make the Stand feel like a white mist dragon-deer covered in flowers.
